purpose: The commit adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device has been unregistered, which would otherwise lead to a use‑after‑free.

The commit deletes an unnecessary inclusion of asm/termios.h from the n_hdlc.c source file. The file already includes linux/tty.h, which brings in the required linux/termios.h, so the asm/termios.h include is redundant. The change simply removes that line, simplifying the include hierarchy.
The commit updates the perf c2c documentation to include the newly added 'N/A' metric for store operations, reflecting changes in the report table. No code behavior is altered.

The patch fixes incorrect handling of the hugetlb vma lock during VMA splitting and range unmapping. It allocates a fresh vma lock for a newly split VMA to avoid double free, adjusts __unmap_hugepage_range_final to free the lock instead of merely clearing VM_MAYSHARE, and tightens the conditions for PMD sharing so that a missing vma lock prevents sharing. These changes resolve a race condition in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio and ensure all VM_MAYSHARE VMAs have a valid lock.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the Realtek rt5682s codec, adding entries for the AVDD and MICVDD power supplies that are already used in a specific board DTS. No functional code changes are made.

The commit adds a missing iounmap() call in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function of the scsi driver, fixing a resource leak where the probe error path frees a mapping but the remove path does not. This prevents leaked I/O memory mappings.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e driver, cleaning up dead code without altering functionality.

The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed, allowing the sentinel value -1 to be used when the key pointer is NULL. This avoids static analysis warnings and aligns the range with the needed -1..3 values.

The commit modifies the RISC-V defconfig to sort the entries of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in the same order that savedefconfig would produce. It does not change any functional behavior, only the ordering of configuration symbols.

The commit adds a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to the MCP251xfd CAN driver and replaces existing inline checks for FD mode with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies the RTC self‑test suite to detect whether an RTC device is present and, if not, skips the test instead of failing it. This prevents false test failures on platforms without RTC hardware.

The commit removes a dead store: an initial assignment to the variable 'bytes' in sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c that is never read because the variable is overwritten later in an if block. This eliminates a clang static analysis warning about dead stores.

The commit modifies the kernel configuration so that the rcuscale feature can be built without requiring RCU Tasks when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE is enabled. This removes an unnecessary dependency, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing.

The commit updates the spte.h header in the KVM x86 MMU code by removing a duplicated phrase "be split" from a comment or macro, simplifying it to a single occurrence. No functional code changes are introduced.

The commit reverts a previous modification that populated the DCN315 clock table using dcfclk values. Because the platform firmware (pmfw) leaves the table empty when dcfclk states are disabled, the dcfclk‑based table caused a "no read"/underflow situation. Reverting the change restores the prior behavior and avoids the underflow until a proper solution is implemented.
The commit updates the device-tree bindings documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC by adding a compatible string that identifies the Mali-G31 Bifrost GPU present on that platform.

The commit renames the struct `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` throughout the DesignWare PCIe core driver to align with the naming convention where all driver‑specific entities use the `dw_` prefix. No functional code changes are introduced; only identifiers are updated for consistency.
The commit updates a printk-style format string in the amd-xgbe driver to use %x instead of %hx, matching the integer promotion rules and silencing a clang -Wformat warning.

The commit modifies the MIPS jump_label code to correct the branch range check. It casts the upper bound to a signed long before comparing, ensuring that negative offsets are handled correctly and the warning is avoided.
The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() in the r8188eu driver and calls the underlying function directly, simplifying the HAL layer.

The commit adds the `static` qualifier to symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c that are only used within the file, eliminating sparse warnings about undeclared symbols. It does not alter functionality, only changes symbol linkage.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for MSM display (MDSS) nodes by removing an interrupt‑flags cell that should not be present because the MDSS interrupt provider supplies only a single cell. This corrects the binding spec to match the hardware behavior.

The commit changes the v4l2 control reference structure to allocate memory for its array members, replacing static or unallocated storage. This prepares the code so that drivers can later modify the array size dynamically.

The commit extends the socinfo driver by adding entries for additional Qualcomm SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and related PMICs, enabling the driver to recognize and report these new platforms.
The commit updates the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver to recognise chip version letters from A to P instead of treating versions beyond E as "unknown", fixing the incorrect handling of newer chip cuts.

The commit removes the .is_enabled state from the PRCMU clock driver in the ux500 platform because the generic clock core already maintains a software enable count, making the custom enable count redundant.

The commit refactors RISC-V memory management initialization code to reduce the number of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives by moving macros closer to related variables and grouping related usages, improving code readability and maintainability.

The commit updates the device tree for the Qualcomm sm6350-lena board on arm64, enabling QUP and GPI DMA hardware blocks so that I2C nodes can be added later. This is a configuration change in the DTS source.

The commit updates comment text in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces an unclear comment with a clearer one, fixes a typo (caliberation → calibration), and adjusts comment formatting to place /* on a separate line, adhering to coding style. No functional code changes are made.

The commit modifies the igc driver’s release_swfw_sync routine to detect failure when acquiring the hardware semaphore, logs an error, and exits the loop instead of looping forever. This addresses a situation where the hardware is surprise‑removed, which previously caused an infinite loop.

The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace old clock‑related defines with the newer ASoC DAI format terminology, aligning the driver with current naming conventions and removing legacy macros.
The commit removes a conditional check (IS_SWAPFILE) in the NFS code that is always true, eliminating dead code that served no functional purpose.

The commit adds handling for GTP echo requests in the kernel driver. When a GTP_ECHO_REQ packet is received, the code now generates a GTP_ECHO_RSP reply containing the appropriate recovery information element and restart counter, complying with the GTP specification for both GTPv0 and GTPv1.

The commit updates the cpumask API usage by replacing calls to cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where the start index is -1 (i.e., start at 0), and introduces a new helper cpumask_first_zero() to provide equivalent functionality. This is a code cleanup and efficiency improvement.

The commit removes the driver’s attempt to query the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR after a station roams, because the firmware no longer supports this operation and the call always fails, producing noisy error messages.
The commit adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) to support the alc5682 headset codec on platforms without a speaker amplifier, connecting it to SSP0 following Intel BT offload design.

The commit adds a validation in the io_uring networking path that makes zerocopy sendmsg fail immediately when the socket does not support zerocopy, aligning its behavior with the existing check for zerocopy send.

The commit updates the documentation build preamble to check whether the file xeCJK.sty exists before attempting to load the xeCJK package. This prevents a LaTeX error on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is not installed, addressing a documented failure on RHEL 9 and similar distributions.

The commit modifies the checkpatch script to stop it from flagging macros that contain a 'case' statement (e.g., '#define C2S(x) case x: return #x') as errors about missing parentheses. This change relaxes a check to accommodate existing driver macros.

The commit replaces the simple‑KMS helper functions in the simpledrm driver with the standard atomic helper functions, removes empty CRTC atomic enable/disable helpers, and forces a call to drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(). No functional behavior is changed; the change is purely architectural/refactoring.

The commit introduces a JPEG decoding worker interface in the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, allowing three hardware decoding units to run in parallel on the MT8195 platform, thereby adding parallel decoding capability.

The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() debug print calls from libata because the same information is already provided by tracepoints, and also deletes the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper function since its output can be obtained via SCSI tracing. This is a cleanup of redundant debugging code.

The commit adds support for entering zstate during the extended vblank period when the driver switches to FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It calculates the extra vblank time, enables zstate if the gain exceeds 5 ms, writes an optimized min_dst_y_next_start value to a register, and restores defaults when exiting FreeSync.

The commit updates the prototype of the hqd_destroy() callback to use the correct enum type for the reset_type parameter, fixing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) type mismatch that caused a kernel crash on systems built with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for STM32 pinctrl. It changes the property name from 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema, tightening validation.

The commit suppresses a GCC 12 array‑bounds warning in the ath9k driver (function ath9k_hw_resettxqueue) by adding a comment or pragma that relegates the warning to a lower level, without altering runtime behavior.
The commit changes the sun8i-ss crypto driver to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync, which simplifies the runtime power‑management code.

The commit moves the fixed‑file installation and removal helper functions out of the io_uring core into the file‑table code and changes their signatures to accept a ctx instead of an io_kiocb, reflecting that they act on the ring itself rather than on a specific request.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, splitting the previously combined G1/G2 decoder node into separate G1 and G2 nodes because they are independent blocks controlled by the vpu‑blk‑ctrl power‑domain driver. It adds the new binding information and notes that the new DT with older kernels is unsupported.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro from the iwlwifi driver configuration code and merges its sole usage directly into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, simplifying the macro definitions.
